title: Sindhu Reaches Japan Open Semifinal After Okuhara Withdrawal
indian_angle: India's top female shuttler, PV Sindhu, has advanced to the semifinals of a major international tournament, boosting hopes for a title.
summary: PV Sindhu secured her spot in the Japan Open semifinals after her opponent, Nozomi Okuhara, withdrew from their quarterfinal match.

title: Sindhu's Dominant Win Over Han Yue Hints at 15-Point Format Advantage
indian_angle: PV Sindhu's strong performance against Han Yue suggests she could thrive under the new 15-point scoring system, which is also being implemented domestically.
summary: PV Sindhu's commanding victory over Han Yue at the Japan Open demonstrated her potential to excel in the proposed 15-point scoring format, highlighting her aggressive style.

title: Junior World Championships 2026: Eligibility Criteria Released for Indian Players
indian_angle: The Badminton Association of India has issued guidelines for the selection of Indian players for the upcoming BWF World Junior Championships in Cairo.
summary: BAI has released the eligibility criteria for Indian players aiming to participate in the BWF World Junior Championships 2026, setting the stage for aspiring young shuttlers.

title: Delhi World Championships 2026: Engaging Gen Z, Alpha Key for Badminton's Future
indian_angle: With India hosting the World Championships in Delhi, there's a focus on attracting younger Indian audiences to secure the sport's long-term popularity.
summary: An article emphasizes the critical need to engage Gen Z and Gen Alpha audiences in India to ensure a vibrant future for badminton, especially as Delhi prepares to host the 2026 World Championships.

title: 3x15 Scoring System to Debut in Indian Domestic Tournaments
indian_angle: A significant change in scoring, the 3x15 system, will be implemented across all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in India, starting soon.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has announced the implementation of the 3x15 scoring system for all domestic ranking tournaments and national championships in the U-13, U-15, and U-17 categories, set to debut in Ernakulam.

title: Chennai to Host All India Senior Ranking Tournament July 16-23
indian_angle: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament is scheduled to take place in Chennai, providing a crucial platform for top Indian shuttlers.
summary: Chennai will host the Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament from July 16th to 23rd, 2026, drawing senior players from across the country for competitive action.
